entries.title: EU Cyber Resilience Act: What It Means for Connected Hardware and IoT Manufacturers
entries.summary: A practical product-security read on the EU Cyber Resilience Act for makers of connected devices, embedded systems, IoT modules, and smart-city hardware: products with digital elements, secure-by-design, lifecycle maintenance, vulnerability handling, support periods, and the 2026–2027 deadlines.

entries.title: Secure by Design Under the CRA: Why Hardware-Backed Trust Matters
entries.summary: The threat model behind the CRA's secure-by-design expectation — cloned devices, exposed keys, insecure provisioning, weak firmware identity, poor lifecycle control — and how a hardware root of trust such as the AmbiSEC module fits a CRA-aligned architecture for connected products.

entries.title: CRA Vulnerability Handling and Product Lifecycle Security: What Manufacturers Need to Prepare
entries.summary: CRA vulnerability handling and lifecycle security in practice: reporting obligations from 11 September 2026, support-period communication, coordinated disclosure, security updates, and how secure elements support key rotation, credential lifecycle, device identity, and controlled updates.

entries.title: Mapping AmbiSecure Products to CRA Readiness: AmbiSEC, ONE Pass, BioKey and Secure Identity
entries.summary: A product-mapping article linking AmbiSecure offerings to CRA-aligned security themes — hardware-backed device identity and secure key storage with AmbiSEC, phishing-resistant access with FIDO and ONE Pass, secure user authentication with BioKey, and secure element-backed credentials.

entries.title: Lava Lamps and Cryptographic Entropy: Inside the Wall of Entropy
entries.summary: How Cloudflare's lava-lamp 'Wall of Entropy' produces randomness — and why hardware TRNGs in secure-element silicon underpin TLS, FIDO, V2X PKI, and eSIM. An engineering-grade walk-through of cryptographic entropy from physical sources through CSPRNG to deployed device keys.

entries.title: How V2X PKI Works: EA, AA, Pseudonymous Certificates, Lifecycle
entries.summary: An engineering walk-through of the V2X PKI: Root CA, Enrolment Authority, Authorisation Authority, Enrolment Credentials, Pseudonymous Certificates, HashedId8 linkage, Butterfly Key Expansion, CRL and CTL distribution. IEEE 1609.2 + ETSI TS 102 941 in practice.

entries.title: Secure Elements in Connected Vehicles: OBU, RSU, and the Hardware Root
entries.summary: Engineering view of the secure element in connected vehicles: key isolation, hardware-bound identity, anti-cloning, secure boot, OTA trust anchors. SIM form factor versus telecom service, removable nano-card versus solderable MFF2, TPM vs HSM vs secure element.

entries.title: Pseudonymous Certificates and Privacy in V2X
entries.summary: Why static V2X identities are dangerous, how Pseudonymous Certificates achieve unlinkability, how Butterfly Key Expansion makes the EA/AA privacy split work in practice, and how revocation propagates without de-anonymising the fleet.

entries.title: Device Identity at Manufacturing Scale
entries.summary: Provisioning device identity at scale: per-device keys inside a secure element on the personalisation line, HSM-backed SCP03 key custody, OTA credential rotation, revocation across connectivity tiers, and V2X / eSIM / IoT lifecycle convergence.

entries.title: Why Software-Only Device Trust Fails
entries.summary: The threat model that rules out software-only device identity in field-deployed hardware: firmware extraction, key cloning, replay, fleet-scale compromise. Software vs TPM vs secure element comparison. Why connected vehicles, industrial IoT, and edge infrastructure converge on hardware-backed identity.

entries.title: Embedded Secure-Element FIDO2 Authenticators for Enterprise Identity
entries.summary: A FIDO2 applet running inside a CC EAL5+ secure element packaged as a removable nano-card (4FF) or a solderable MFF2 module. Loaded under the issuer's keys, not the device-vendor's. One piece of silicon, one personalisation line, one revocation surface.

entries.title: PIV Smart Cards vs USB Tokens vs Embedded Secure Elements
entries.summary: Workforce identity credential matrix. Lifecycle, physical-logical convergence, certificate workflows, deployment realities for three hardware-backed classes.

entries.title: Designing Secure Email and Document Signing Platforms
entries.summary: Architecture for S/MIME email signing, PDF/PKCS#7 document signing, hardware-backed credentials, Long-Term Validation, and the trust-list problem.

entries.title: Building Secure IoT Identity with Security Applets
entries.summary: Five JavaCard applets for IoT device identity on a CC EAL5+ secure element: provisioning, attestation, mTLS, signed firmware, key rotation.

entries.title: Engineering ePassport Issuance and Identity Platforms
entries.summary: Architecture of an ICAO 9303 ePassport platform. CSCA/DSC PKI, LDS generation, SOd signing, enrolment frontend, personalisation backbone, inspection-system reference.

entries.title: How to Choose Between Smart Cards, FIDO Tokens and Passkeys
entries.summary: Decision-grade comparison of smart-card authenticators, dedicated FIDO security keys, and passkeys. Threat model, lifecycle, recovery.

entries.title: Secure Element vs TPM vs HSM — Where Each Fits
entries.summary: Three classes of hardware key-storage everyone confuses. What each is for and what it refuses to do.

entries.title: Designing Secure Credential Lifecycle Management
entries.summary: Credentials don't just get issued — they get rotated, recovered, revoked. The operations-grade view.

entries.title: Why Transit Validators Need Offline Trust Architecture
entries.summary: Closed-loop transit ticketing has to keep collecting fares when the backend is unreachable.

entries.title: JavaCard Applet Development for Enterprise Identity
entries.summary: What shipping a JavaCard applet actually involves: AID design, lifecycle, secure messaging, personalisation.

entries.title: PKI Credential Issuance for Workforce and Government Identity
entries.summary: RA, CA, key custody, attestation, lifecycle, audit. The PKI architecture decisions you can't put off.

entries.title: How FIDO Authentication Works
entries.summary: A clear explanation of FIDO2 / WebAuthn — registration, authentication, origin binding — without spec apologetics.

entries.title: Where Your AmbiSecure FIDO Key Works
entries.summary: Current, vendor-neutral overview of the platforms and services accepting FIDO2 / WebAuthn.

entries.title: Cyber Security Threats — What Actually Matters in 2026
entries.summary: A clear-eyed overview of the threats driving identity and hardware-credential decisions today.

entries.title: Implementing FIDO2 Authentication — A Complete Developer Guide
entries.summary: A practical, code-first walk through FIDO2 registration and authentication, attestation, and the bits people get wrong.

entries.title: Designing Enterprise Passwordless Systems
entries.summary: What an end-to-end enterprise rollout looks like — IdP integration, recovery, mixed authenticator fleets, AAL3 architecture.

entries.title: Passkeys vs Traditional MFA
entries.summary: Where passkeys actually replace MFA, where they don't, and how to think about device-bound vs synced credentials in enterprise.

entries.title: Platform vs Roaming Authenticators
entries.summary: When to standardise on the Yubikey-style external authenticator vs the device-resident platform authenticator. Tradeoffs.

entries.title: Understanding WebAuthn Attestation Objects
entries.summary: Walk a real attestation object byte by byte. AAGUID, COSE keys, attestation statement formats, and what to verify.

entries.title: Why Hardware-Backed Identity Matters
entries.summary: What hardware-bound credentials buy you that software ones don't. Threat models, attestation, deployment realism.

entries.title: APDU From First Principles
entries.summary: ISO 7816-4 APDUs explained: CLA, INS, P1/P2, Lc, data, Le, status words. With real DESFire/JavaCard examples.

entries.title: DESFire EV1 vs EV2 vs EV3
entries.summary: How the DESFire family has evolved, what changed at each tier, and how to reason about legacy vs modern deployments.

entries.title: Designing Low-Latency Secure Transit Validators
entries.summary: Why transit validators need offline trust, sub-300ms response, and SAM-backed attestation. With real numbers.

entries.title: Why SAMs Matter in Closed-Loop Transit
entries.summary: Secure Access Modules in transit reader design, when to use them, and where they earn their cost.

entries.title: Top 3 Benefits of MFA
entries.summary: A clear-eyed look at what MFA actually buys you in 2026 — and where it doesn't help.

entries.title: Why Use Multi-Factor Authentication
entries.summary: An updated, technically-grounded answer to why MFA is still worth the friction.

entries.title: Common Misconceptions about Two-Factor Authentication
entries.summary: Unpacks the most common misconceptions about two-factor authentication — what 2FA actually defends against, where SMS-based codes fall short, and why hardware-rooted second factors hold up under credential-theft attacks.

entries.title: Single Sign-On Vs. Multi-Factor Authentication
entries.summary: Compares single sign-on with multi-factor authentication — when each model fits, where they overlap, and how SSO + MFA combine for enterprise identity.

entries.title: Multi-factor Authentication in Government Sector
entries.summary: Why government identity programmes are adopting multi-factor authentication, how MFA holds up against phishing and credential theft, and where hardware-rooted authenticators sit in the stack.

entries.title: Cyber Attacks in India – Part 3
entries.summary: Part three of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India — closing the series with systemic gaps in incident response, identity hygiene, and hardware-rooted authentication adoption.

entries.title: Cyber Attacks in India – Part 2
entries.summary: Part two of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India — the attack patterns, the identity weaknesses they exploited, and what enterprise authentication posture would have stopped them.

entries.title: Cyber Attacks in India
entries.summary: Part one of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India and what they reveal about systemic gaps in online identity, authentication, and incident response.

entries.title: Enterprise Security Threats
entries.summary: A field overview of enterprise security threats in the cloud era — how the digital attack surface expands as organisations adopt new platforms, and where hardware-rooted identity narrows it.

entries.title: What is Passwordless Authentication?
entries.summary: A primer on passwordless authentication — what it actually means, how FIDO and biometrics replace shared secrets, and why hardware-bound credentials sit at the centre of the model.

entries.title: Is Passwordless the future?
entries.summary: Examines whether passwordless authentication can scale beyond pilots — the standards, attack-surface trade-offs, and operational realities that decide its trajectory.

entries.title: SMS-based OTP Authentication and Its Disadvantages
entries.summary: Why SMS-based one-time passwords are no longer a defensible second factor — SIM-swap attacks, SS7 weaknesses, phishing-prone OTP relay, and the hardware-rooted alternatives.

entries.title: Taking security future for Government
entries.summary: How governments are moving from password-only access to multi-factor authentication for citizen and employee identity — and where hardware-rooted credentials reshape the threat model.

entries.title: E-passport and How will chip-based e-Passports work
entries.summary: How chip-based ePassports work in practice — the BAC / PACE access protocols, the CSCA / DSC / PKD trust chain, and the role of the secure element inside the document.

entries.title: E-passport and Its Application
entries.summary: How biometric passports work in practice — chip-based identity, border-control architecture, and the standards stack that keeps the issuer chain trustworthy.

entries.title: An Introduction to Java Card Technology
entries.summary: An introduction to JavaCard — how Java-based applets run securely on smart cards and other small-memory secure elements, why the JavaCard runtime model exists, and what it enables for FIDO, PIV, and ePassport applets.

entries.title: Fast Identity Online (FIDO)
entries.summary: A primer on FIDO (Fast Identity Online) and the Universal Second Factor model — how FIDO replaces shared secrets with hardware-bound credentials and why it changes the phishing-resistance baseline.

entries.title: Understanding EMV certification In Public Transportation
entries.summary: A practical look at EMV certification in public transport — which certifications apply, the roles of acquirer, scheme, terminal vendor, and transit authority, and the typical timeline from prototype to revenue service.

entries.title: Workplace Security — How Biometrics Is the Key to the New Normal
entries.summary: How workplace biometrics layer additional security onto existing authentication methods — face / fingerprint / palm, the privacy controls that keep them defensible, and where they sit alongside FIDO and MFA.

entries.title: Consumer Biometrics in the Data Privacy Age
entries.summary: How organisations can deploy biometric authentication while still meeting modern privacy expectations — match-on-device versus match-on-server, template storage, and the regulatory shape of consumer biometric data.

entries.title: Securing your IIoT infrastructure
entries.summary: Why industrial IoT infrastructure remains a high-value target — the markets driving adoption, the trust gaps in legacy OT, and how hardware-rooted device identity tightens them.

entries.title: Public Transport Ticketing System (Part-3)
entries.summary: Part three of the AFC series — comparative notes across the analysed countries, what was common in mature ticketing architectures, and where hardware-rooted secure access modules sit in the stack.

entries.title: Public Transport Ticketing System (Part-2)
entries.summary: Part two of the AFC series — how Ambimat Electronics has approached automated fare collection: validators, SAM-backed offline trust, and revenue assurance.

entries.title: Public Transport Ticketing System (Part-1)
entries.summary: First of three articles on automated fare collection — a comparative look at ticketing architectures across advanced economies and large transit networks in Asia.

entries.title: Challenges to IoT Security (Part 2)
entries.summary: Second of a two-part look at IoT security — practical mitigations: hardware root of trust, signed firmware update, attestation, and device identity carried in a secure element.

entries.title: Challenges to IoT Security (Part 1)
entries.summary: First of a two-part look at IoT security — how billions of devices communicate with enterprise systems, where the resulting attack surface sits, and the trust anchors that contain it.
